hooks.command: dir="$(pwd)"; hash="$(echo -n "$dir" | md5)"; pidfile="/tmp/jcmwatch-${hash}.pid"; if [ -f "$pidfile" ]; then pid=$(awk '{print $1}' "$pidfile"); stored_start=$(cut -d' ' -f2- "$pidfile"); actual_start=$(ps -p "$pid" -o lstart= 2>/dev/null | tr -s ' '); if [ "$stored_start" = "$actual_start" ]; then echo '{"systemMessage": "jcodemunch is available and jcmwatch is running — index is kept current automatically. Use resolve_repo, search_symbols, get_file_outline, find_references, get_context_bundle for code exploration."}'; else echo '{"systemMessage": "jcodemunch is available. Use resolve_repo, search_symbols, get_file_outline, find_references, get_context_bundle — NOT Grep/Read — for code exploration. jcmwatch is NOT running; run jcmindex if the index may be stale."}'; fi; else echo '{"systemMessage": "jcodemunch is available. Use resolve_repo, search_symbols, get_file_outline, find_references, get_context_bundle — NOT Grep/Read — for code exploration. jcmwatch is NOT running; run jcmindex if the index may be stale."}'; fi
echo '{"continue": false, "stopReason": "Grep and Glob are blocked. Use jcodemunch tools instead: search_symbols, get_file_outline, find_references, or get_context_bundle."}'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "additionalContext": "REMINDER: Prefer jcodemunch tools (search_symbols, get_file_outline, find_references, get_context_bundle) over Read for code exploration. Only use Read for targeted reads of known file locations or editing workflows."}}'
